name: Mixed health — schedule behind, budget OK, 3 red risks
description: Project with SPI=0.88 (schedule behind), CPI=1.02 (budget slightly under), and 3 high-severity open risks. Tests dimension-assessor's ability to correctly flag schedule as Red/Amber while keeping budget Green, and risk dimension as Red. Recovery-recommender should suggest schedule recovery strategies (fast-tracking or scope reduction) without jeopardizing the healthy budget position.

expected_output.schedule_rag: Amber or Red — SPI 0.88 is below 0.95 threshold
expected_output.budget_rag: Green — CPI 1.02 is within healthy range
expected_output.risk_rag: Red — 3 open high risks with only 45% mitigation completion
expected_output.composite_health: Amber at best — Red override rule may apply due to risk dimension
expected_output.recovery_actions: Schedule: fast-tracking or scope reduction; Risk: accelerate mitigation plans, escalate unmitigated risks
expected_output.must_not: Must NOT recommend budget cuts or crashing (which would hurt the healthy budget position)

name: Team burnout — 95% utilization sustained 3 sprints
description: Project showing team burnout signals: 95% utilization for 3 consecutive sprints, rising overtime, increasing unplanned absences, and ceremony skip rate climbing. Tests early-warning-detector's ability to identify the burnout pattern and trend-correlator's ability to predict downstream quality and risk degradation.

expected_output.team_rag: Red — sustained 95% utilization with rising absences and overtime is a clear burnout pattern
early_warnings: Velocity declining (-1.8σ from baseline over 4 sprints)
Overtime trending up (+2σ, tripled in 4 sprints)
Retrospective action completion dangerously low at 40%
trend_correlations: Team utilization -> quality degradation (defect density rising in lockstep with utilization)
Team burnout -> velocity decline (predicted schedule impact in 2-3 sprints)
Knowledge silo concentration -> risk amplification (single points of failure)
expected_output.recovery_actions: Immediate utilization cap at 85%, redistribute workload, cancel non-critical ceremonies to free capacity, address knowledge silos through pairing, consider temporary staff augmentation
expected_output.distress_probability: High (>75%) — multiple correlated burnout signals sustained over 3+ sprints

name: All-green project — validate healthy state across all dimensions
description: A well-performing project with all dimensions in Green. Tests that the agents correctly validate a healthy state, do not generate false alarms, and provide a concise 'maintain course' recommendation rather than unnecessary recovery actions.

expected_output.all_dimensions_rag: Green — every dimension within healthy thresholds
expected_output.early_warnings: None — no signals exceed Watch threshold
expected_output.trend_correlations: No concerning correlations — positive reinforcement loop (good team health -> good quality -> stable schedule)
expected_output.recovery_actions: None required — maintain current practices, document what is working well for portfolio-level lessons learned
expected_output.must_not: Must NOT generate false alarms, unnecessary recovery plans, or suggest changes to a healthy project

name: Scope creep cascade — correlating scope growth with schedule and budget degradation
description: Project experiencing uncontrolled scope creep that is cascading into schedule slip and budget overrun. Tests trend-correlator's ability to identify the scope-schedule-budget causal chain and recovery-recommender's ability to prescribe scope control as the root-cause intervention rather than treating symptoms.

expected_output.scope_rag: Red — 14 open CRs, 22% requirements volatility, 8 features added with 0 removed, 4 baseline changes
expected_output.schedule_rag: Red — SPI degraded from 1.02 to 0.87 over 4 sprints, 18 days behind
expected_output.budget_rag: Red — CPI degraded from 1.01 to 0.89, EAC 12.5% over BAC
trend_correlations: Scope creep (r=0.95) driving schedule degradation — CR volume increase precedes SPI decline by 1 sprint
Scope creep (r=0.92) driving budget overrun — unplanned features consuming unplanned budget
Schedule pressure (r=0.88) driving team overtime — overtime tripled as SPI declined
Secondary: governance decay enabling scope creep — change control compliance at 45%
expected_output.root_cause: Uncontrolled scope growth due to weak change control governance (45% compliance) and low sponsor availability (40%)
recovery_actions: Immediate scope freeze — no new CRs accepted until backlog of 14 is triaged
Emergency change control board session to approve/reject/defer all 14 open CRs
Restore change control compliance to >90% — enforce governance gate
Negotiate scope reduction: identify 3-5 low-value features for deferral to Phase 2
Increase sponsor availability to >70% to clear decision backlog of 7 items
Only AFTER scope is controlled: reassess schedule and budget recovery options
expected_output.must_not: Must NOT recommend crashing or fast-tracking as primary actions — those treat symptoms; scope control is the root cause intervention
